title: Phyllorkis monantha
taxonomy: Phyllorkis monantha is classified within the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Orchidoideae. It belongs to the genus Phyllorkis and is further categorized as a species within this genus. The species Phyllorkis monantha is native to the tropical regions of Asia. This classification suggests that Phyllorkis monantha is a type of orchid, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants. The classification of Phyllorkis monantha is based on its botanical characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants.

Its roots and leaves are reportedly used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. intro: Phyllorkis monantha, also known as the single-flowered Phyllorkis, is a plant species in the orchid family (Orchidaceae) native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms and promote recovery. Bioactive constituents of Phyllorkis monantha include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Phyllorkis monantha is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create remedies for respiratory and infectious diseases.
